candidates.stmt:
I’m south Essex born and a Billericay resident for thirty plus years.  In that time, I’ve been a long-serving local school governor, chaired the Essex Wildlife Trust and completed two terms as the Honorary Secretary of the Royal Society of Wildlife Trusts. So it’s no shock that I know just how vital all of our open green spaces are. 
I currently work for the Professional Footballers' Association, supporting former players living with dementia. I’m also a local football club director.
Like most local residents, I'm deeply concerned about the way that mass housing development is being foisted on our town without care or concern for local people.  Our schools, GP and other health services, roads and public transport are already at breaking point.  Without massive improvements in local infrastructure, our town will cease to function properly.  We simply cannot allow this to continue as it is.
I’m grateful to have been selected as a Green candidate for my town again - and together we can build a real voice for Billericay..

I am currently Essex County Councillor for Bocking Division, having previously served on Braintree District Council for 36 years. The most important thing to me is to represent residents’ views and do what I can for them, along with a wealth of experience of knowing how the council works.

Paul Thorogood is the Green Party candidate for Braintree constituency at the General Election to be held on Thursday 4th July 2024.

Paul grew up in the Cressing Road area of Braintree, and attended the Alec Hunter High School (now Academy) before gaining a 2.1 Honours degree in Politics from Southampton University.

Paul has an NCTJ professional qualification in journalism and worked as a journalist for 23 years. He started his journalism career at the Braintree & Witham Times before moving on to the Colchester Gazette, Ipswich Evening Star and several Fleet Street titles including The Daily Mirror when Piers Morgan was editor.

Paul now runs his own small business and lives in Coggeshall and is married with two children who attend local state schools. He became involved in local politics when plans for the Rivenhall Airfield waste incinerator were gaining traction and he began reading about the subject. Now a committed environmentalist, Paul is concerned with protecting and preserving the natural environment from pollution and overdevelopment. He believes strongly in the principle ‘Think globally, act locally’ - globally campaigning against climate change; locally improving services such as schools, saving libraries from closure and protecting wildlife and the countryside.

Paul was elected as a Braintree District Councillor to represent Kelvedon & Feering ward in 2019 and then re-elected in 2023. He was elected as the County Councillor for Braintree Eastern division in 2021.

Witham Liberal Democrats have selected Ashley Thompson as their Parliamentary Candidate to fight the Tory incumbent Priti Patel at the forthcoming General Election. Ashley has lived virtually his entire life in Essex, having attended Southend High School For Boys and University of Essex, before embarking on a career in classical music Ashley spent a year as a Choral Scholar to Chelmsford Cathedral and now directs Witham Choral Society and, from April 2024, St. Nicolas Parish Church, Witham. He is a Lib Dem Councillor at Chelmsford City Council.

His work as an educationalist has led him to believe strongly in the need for better mental health support in schools. The Lib Dems are pushing for a state funded mental health support worker for EVERY school in the UK. This will be funded by tripling taxes on the big social media companies, who are so culpable in exacerbating body image and self esteem issues in our young people.

The state of the NHS is another top concern for Ashley, who believes we need to adopt an Invest to Save approach, because out of control and ever rising waiting lists are a total false economy. He is bewildered by the NHS real term cuts of £1.3billion planned for 2024/25 - money which could instead fund 31 million GP appointments!

Ashley says: "It's time the people of Witham stopped being taken for granted and had a proper voice. I've seen first hand the true extent of the toll that endless Tory cuts have had on youth services, child and adult social care, mental health services and most immediately visible of all, local highways. I have experienced at close quarters the effects of the cost of living and housing crises on everyone, but most especially the young, and am more than prepared to fight vested interests in an effort to create a fairer and greener society for all."

Terence was born in Colchester and spent his first 10 years moving about with his family as his father was a soldier. After returning to Colchester he completed his secondary education and then worked on a building site for a year before joining the Army himself to fulfil an ambition to train as a Mechanical Engineer.

After 25 years’ service he left the Army having attained the rank of Warrant Officer 2nd Class and now works as an Engineering Operations Manager maintaining plant for wastewater sites.

His current role has given him an insight into the pollution that is sent out into our waterways and beaches by outdated infrastructure which is unable to cope with the increased number of houses connected to it.  He wants water companies to be forced to reinvest their profits on equipment instead of just paying fines.

He decided to stand as a candidate for Reform UK because he found that the Con/Lab/Lib wheel wasn't representing the wishes and needs of our country, with the career politicians failing to keep the promises that had won them elections, showing contempt for the electorate. Someone had to take a stand to give the people of Colchester an alternative to vote for, so he stood up to the task.

Terence has been married to Sharon for 32 years and they have 3 children and one grandson. They both enjoy going out on dog walks or riding their motorbikes around the County’s lanes, dodging the potholes and stopping off for refreshments along the way.

I am standing as your Green Party candidate for Braintree Town, to make sure local people’s voices are heard and respected. I will push for proper support for children and young people with special educational needs, stand up for minority groups and LGBTQ+ rights. Fight to protect our environment for future generations. I believe companies must be held to account for pollution, not let off the hook. Our town centre deserves better transport links and fairer parking charges, so that it can truly flourish. We need genuinely affordable homes for local people, not just luxury homes for the few, we should build on unused and under used industrial land — not by sacrificing our green spaces. As your councillor for Braintree Town, I will put people, equality and the environment at the heart of all my efforts.

Richard has been a dedicated parish councillor since 2015, a city councillor in The Lawns since 2019, member of the local Speedwatch group and is an active volunteer for the Chelmsford Litter Wombles & the SUPsect Water Wombles, helping keep our city clean and tidy.

He is committed to community safety, fixing the pothole crisis and listening to residents’ concerns. Alongside Mike, he has continuously stood up for Springfield residents over many years.

Richard will be a strong local champion for families and communities in Springfield. On 18th June, vote for Richard Lee and the Lib Dems.

I’m standing to be a a representative to the local community on local issues in the forthcoming Hutton South by-election.

Under the Conservatives and Liberal Democrats in Brentwood we’ve seen council tax increase by 46% in the past ten years yet services have suffered. The council even intends to reduce our bin collections to fortnightly and now charges us extra to recycle garden waste. Our roads are strewn with potholes and our High Street shops are increasingly boarded up.

Crime has soared under the Conservative Police and Crime Commissioner. He sits on the local council. Detection rates have plummeted to the extent that criminals committing the most serious sexual crimes in Essex have barely a 3% chance of being prosecuted now.

The Conservative Lib Dem council is now planning to take a bulldozer to our countryside and is keen to build a huge new town within our borough with no consultation amongst local people.

I will fight against all of these failures and expose the huge financial waste that exists within our local authority.

The Lib Dem Tory uni party is not fit for purpose here. I’ll be a loud voice for Hutton South, the area in which I’ve lived for years, as I was when i was a Brentwood councillor a decade ago.

If there is one positive the Coronavirus has taught us, it is this: caring, vibrant, local community life is essential; essential for our quality of life and, at times, essential for our very survival.

The importance of community was clear throughout my 45-year career in education, and it could not be clearer during this global epidemic.

I have lived in Brentwood for more than 33 years and the town has everything going for it.

There are parks, woodland and open spaces within minutes of the High Street. It has history as a route centre since the days of the Romans and the Pilgrims, and is within easy reach of London and the coast. Greens want to revive communities like ours. Give Greens the chance!

Susan and her husband have lived in Warley since 1997. She taught French and German in comprehensive schools for 30 years. Now retired, she works voluntarily as a community adviser, supporting Brentwood residents with issues such as housing, employment problems or benefits.

She loves walking and cycling, and enjoys music and the arts. Genuinely affordable housing is desperately needed here. Susan will prioritise bringing empty houses back into use, giving first refusal to local first-time buyers on new homes and ensuring that developments include social housing and property for older people. She is also keen to require private landlord accreditation to provide decent accommodation at reasonable rents and to increase security by encouraging longer-term tenancies.

Cuts in Council funding threaten the very existence of community groups. Susan will work to secure long-term, stable funding for groups like Mind, Brentwood Community Transport, Citizens Advice and our Council for Voluntary Services.

I moved to Ingatestone 30 years ago and taught in a secondary school in Brentwood for 20 years. As a Parish Councillor I am heavily involved in my local community and this involvement also extends to being Chair of Ingatestone & Fryerning Cricket Club.

I joined the Green Party in 2011 and was selected to be our parliamentary candidate in Brentwood in both 2017 and 2019 general elections.

The last year has shown the vital importance of local communities. We are fortunate to have access to green space, but it must be defended against unsustainable development.

Both Brentwood and Essex councils need Green councillors to raise the issues that other parties often ignore. The climate and ecological crisis that we face needs addressing without delay - a vote for the Green Party is a vote for climate action now.

Richard has lived and worked in Brentwood since 1990 in the local university and as governor for two schools. He acts as a consultant on education and technology as well as leading research in the field. His concerns are that education is badly underfunded, that it should have democratic overview and that the academy and free school arrangements have lead to overpaid chief executives of unaccountable trusts. This affects local residents by making it easy for schools to drop support for a wide curriculum, leaving out the creative subjects and opportunities for children with special needs.

If elected Richard  will work hard to sort out the housing crisis in Brentwood, which leaves many young people unable to afford a home and will pursue education issues working with the County Council.

I have lived in Brentwood most of my life, having moved here with my parents as a very young child

I have always voted, but over the last few years I have become increasingly disillusioned with the incompetence, lies, and broken promises of the prevailing government, and the current party of opposition just offers more of the same. Britain deserves better. Reform UK offer a proper genuine alternative to the current two-party system.

I believe that Britain cannot sustain the massive immigration numbers that we have seen over the last few years. I believe that the race towards net zero is hitting the poorest in society first and ultimately will lead to us all being colder and poorer. I believe in a low regulation low taxation economy that encourages free market economics, in a society where the rights of criminals do not trump the rights of victims, and where children should be protected and the elderly respected.

We managed to find £37 billion to pay for a failed test track and trace system during Covid, yet fail to adequately fund our NHS, Social Care, Education or Criminal Justice system. Taxation is the highest it has been in over 70 years, and government overreach is out of control. This is all wrong. Westminster seems to have forgotten that the government exists because of the people; the people do not exist because of the government.

When I discovered Reform UK, I found a party that shared many of my views. Standing as a political candidate was never on my “to do” list, but circumstances change.

I applied and was accepted to stand for Reform UK at the next General Election, giving the people of Brentwood and Ongar an alternative to the current two-party system. It’s time for people to stop voting for the least worst option, and start voting for what they believe in.

I’m standing as an independent candidate for Essex County Council because I believe local elections should be about getting things done, not party politics.

I live and work in the area, and like many residents I see the same issues every day.

My focus is simple: fix the basics and represent the area properly.

That means:

🛣️ Improving roads, pavements and drainage
💡 Fixing street lighting and local infrastructure
🚌 Supporting better, more reliable public transport
🏫 Improving SEND provision and support for families
🌿 Protecting green spaces and making better use of brownfield land

I’m not part of any political party, so I won’t be taking instructions or voting along party lines. My only focus will be what is best for Brightlingsea, Alresford, Great Bentley, Elmstead Market and Thorrington.

I’ve spent years getting stuck into local issues, from setting up Community Speedwatch and traffic monitoring, to organising volunteer groups to clear footpaths and improve access.

If elected, I will:

Be visible and accessible
Take responsibility for issues and follow them through
Challenge where needed and work with others where it gets results

This election is about who will actually represent the area and get things sorted.

If that’s what you’re looking for, I’d really appreciate your support.

Born in St. John's Hospital, I have lived in Chelmsford all my life, attending Barnes Farm Primary School in Chelmer Village. I joined the Green Party in early 2018; increasingly concerned with the area's issues with the environment, poverty and inequality I joined the only party that made sense to me.

I am a keen squash player, and enjoyed playing football when I was younger

Chelmsford has a lot going for it, countryside within walking distance and in normal times a vibrant city centre - but it needs pointing in the right direction. There is a lot of ‘greenwashing’ from the other parties, but if you truly Want Green - Vote Green!

Mike Steel is Chelmsford City Cllr for the Broomfield and Walthams ward and Chairman of Great Waltham Parish Council. He has lived in the Chelmsford area for the last 40 years, and currently resides in Great Waltham 

Mike Steel moved to the Chelmsford area 41 years ago after completing a degree in Electronic Engineering in Leeds. He joined the Marconi company where he worked for the next 20 years, followed by 20 years at BT. His last role before retiring in 2018, was  managing a $1Bn global IT contract for the Pharmaceuticals industry. His background is technology and business and he is a Charted Engineer.

Over the 41 years he has lived in central Chelmsford, Broomfield and latterly Great Waltham. He is well known throughout the constituency – 25 years as a Parish Cllr, 7 years as Chairman, City Cllr, Scout Chairman, and a member of many committees – Twinning, British Legion, Road Traffic, Speedwatch, Parish Productions, Housing Liaison. He is also a qualified first aider and volunteers for many local events.   

Mike involves himself in many ECC aspects, being the Superfast Broadband Champion for the area and a Local Highways Panel member. During the 1st wave of Covid-19, Mike and his wife, Joanna, set up a volunteer group registered with Essex Welfare Services and Chelmsford Volunteer Services. He is also a Goodsam responder. 

Mike has 3 children all of which attended the local primary and secondary schools, before going on to University and successful careers. He is an active gym user and 3rd Duan karate blackbelt instructor.

Our candidate for Broomfield and Writtle & for the by-election in Broomfield and The Walthams is Rachel Wheelhouse 👍

Rachel has lived in Broomfield for over 20 years, raised a family here and also worked at Broomfield Hospital, so is a true local champion. She is passionate about sorting potholes and pavements being accessible for all users, the environment, dealing with antisocial behaviour and championing local businesses and talent in arts and music.

On 7th May, vote for Rachel and the Lib Dems for a community champion ready to take on Tories and Reform.

**Dear Resident**,

My name is Kevin, and I’m standing for you in Broomfield & Writtle division and Broomfield & The Walthams ward.

I live in Broomfield and work locally as a paramedic. Through my work, I see first-hand the pressures on our roads, services and community— I understand how much it matters that we feel safe, connected, and able to enjoy the natural world around us.

**A practical plan for our area**

I will focus on issues that really matter:

· Housing development that starts with services—schools, GP access, buses, and green spaces, and working with police and community safety partners

· Bus-first planning, protecting routes (including evenings and Sundays) and properly linking communities

· Safer school journeys, including a 20mph zone on School Lane and measures to reduce rat-running

· Proper road maintenance—because prevention is cheaper than repair, and residents shouldn’t pay twice

· Stronger support for social care, with reliable, well-commissioned services rather than patchwork provision

· Protection of wildlife and access to nature as a core part of planning decisions

· Transparency in local government and meaningful consultation—so residents are informed and have a real say in the decisions that affect them.

**A note on this election**

As the Green Party is largely funded by members and small donations, we have the freedom to stay focused on local priorities. By contrast, larger parties such as the Conservatives—and newer parties like Reform—rely heavily on large donors and corporations. I often urge voters to consider what interests those funding models could bring with them.

I notice too, that there has been some focus diverted to national issues like immigration. I want to be straight with voters: local councils do not set immigration policy. These elections are about our services, planning, transport, and community wellbeing. Suggesting otherwise risks distracting from the decisions that truly affect our day-to-day life here.

Councillor Nigel Betts - Burnham-on-Crouch resident & Town Councillor. 
I am married to Trudy, blessed with 2 wonderful sons & 2 gorgeous grandchildren. My wife, Trudy, went to school in "the clock tower" & grew up in Burnham. We have many family members living in the Dengie. I have known & loved this area for 40 years.
I am passionate about my local community & the unique charm of Burnham-on-Crouch & Southminster. I will prioritise the needs of local residents whilst ensuring that I leverage my experience for the maximum benefit of our community.

My priorities for Burstead:
1. Fight against overdevelopment 
2. Oppose merger with Thurrock
3. Restore free parking
4. Lower taxes
5. Better health provision

Keiron was, by his own admission, politically homeless until 2021, having never had an active role in politics or association with any party. He joined Reform UK in 2021 after finding so many similarities with his own political beliefs that he decided to stand himself.

Since then Keiron has been a regular & visible presence in Castle Point, and is encouraged by the growing local support.

Keiron has worked within sales in different industries ever since he turned 21, since his first-ever job at Burger King in Southend, followed by 2 summers spent at Adventure Island! During 2020 he was to be found delivering pizzas most evenings in Rayleigh when everything else shut down.

Far from being all work and no play, Keiron is an avid F1, Indycar and football fan & has recently become a regular fan watching the Chelmsford Chieftains ice hockey team. 

Keiron is happy to answer any queries you have at any time, so please do not hesitate to contact him. And he is committed to serving Castle Point to the very best of his ability.

Dear Resident,
May I take a few moments of your time to introduce myself to you.

My name is Dave Blackwell and I have represented you on the council for the last 24 years and as a life long Islander   I am very proud that so many of you have elected me to    represent you over the years.

I have been working tirelessly to resolve the main issues which affect you as you go about your daily lives. I have pushed hard to get many roads in this area resurfaced and footpaths reinstated but also, I appreciate there is still a long way to go.

I have been working with all the agencies to help resolve the surface water flooding issues on Canvey too. Working alongside the police to help cut the crime on Canvey.

I believe we need to continue our strong independent voice on Canvey and we, the Canvey Island Independent Party can speak out your concerns and stand up against the mainland Tory Councillors who don't seem to care what happens on our beloved Island.

Canvey has many issues and with your vote I would like to carry on fighting to help resolve them. Crime, antisocial   behaviour, road infrastructure, over development and many, many more issues that both you and I face each day.

Kind Regards
Dave Blackwell

Dear Residents

Having been a member of the Canvey community for most of my life I have seen many changes to our island. Some have been good. Some have been bad.

I have found representing the people of Canvey Island an honour. We have great organisations and community groups that do many wonderful things for our island, and it has been a pleasure meeting and working with them.

All of my family live on Canvey Island, my mother brother sister, my children, and grandchildren.

My pledge to you is I will always fight for Canvey Island and its residents for issues that concern you.

Thank you

FLOOD RISK MANAGEMENT!
TACKLE THE TRAFFIC!
COMBAT OVER-DEVELOPMENT
SAY NO TO UP TO 3,500 MORE HOUSES AND FLATS ON. CANVEY!

My name is Paul Harbord and I am asking you to please support me with your vote so I can fight for you on Essex County Council.

I am a husband, a proud father of two.  I have spent most of my life managing facilities for property companies.  I am now semi-retired and work as a handyman around the Island.  I always speak my mind and I believe in hard graft.

Both Essex County Councillors for Canvey are part of the same ruling independent party group running the borough and town councils.  They are not making the right decisions for Canvey.
- why have they not ensured that flood risk management is a top priority?
- why have they not made best use of the members’ scheme to get potholes and road improvements carried out, making travel around the Island faster?
- and why have they allowed the decisions for 3,000+ homes to be allocated to Canvey in the Local Plan?

Flood Risk Management - I will put pressure on the Borough Council, water companies and the Environment Agency to improve flood risk management and surface water controls on the Island, including the Lake and ensure areas like the RSPB reserve are properly managed to allow for safe management of potential flood events.  I would also like to see more trees planted on the Island which can also help with flood events.

Tackle the Traffic - Working on the Island, I understand the frustration of getting from A to B.  I will therefore campaign for better infrastructure and traffic management schemes, campaigning for the third Road including the completion of Roscommon Way and a new access road via the Manor away to the A13.

Combat Over-Development - Canveys infrastructure is at breaking point, I will push for a common sense approach to local planning.  I share residents concerns about the 3,000+ homes included in CPBC’s Local Plan.  This is clearly disproportionate to the space available on th

My wife Ruth and I moved to Canvey Island from London over fifty years ago, I have a son and daughter both of whom went to school on Canvey and I also have two grandchildren. I was self employed for most of my working life, for many years Ruth and I run a small printing business and in later years I worked as a Castle Point taxi driver.

In the 1970’s I was actively involved with the Castle Point Refineries Resistance Group protesting against dangerous installations on Canvey Island. In the 1990’s I was politically active in The Socialist Party and the Socialist Alliance and stood in local elections for both. In the 2020’s I was actively involved in the Campaign Against Arms Trade and served on the steering committee for two years.

I also become involved with the Peace Pledge Union and have pledged my support believing that War is a crime against humanity

In 2007 Ruth and I with a small number of very good and generous friends started the Socialist Diners Club and raised over £20.000 which we distributed to local, national and international organizations. I am currently the treasurer of Southend Keep Our NHS Public. 

I am a supporting member of Liberty and some time ago I was contacted by them because two people had nominated me to stand as a candidate for their campaign committee. I regrettably declined due to other commitments. Ruth always took an interest in environmental issues and joined the Green Party years before me.

In her last years she expressed serious climate concerns and greatly influenced my thinking. I am now committed to the argument that for a great many years’ government short term polices, party interest and the casual indifference that the future is someone else’s problem has contributed to today’s serious problems that our children and their children will have to deal with. In truth I am comparatively new to green politics but most people are recycling, aware of what is happening around the world and of the future weather predictions.

All

Stewart will be a strong, fresh, independent voice for residents across Castledon & Crouch from Noak Bridge in the West to Wickford in the East.

Stewart pledges to be a visible and accessible Councillor working hard all year round, not just at election time. Stewart will campaign for a greener, fairer and safer Borough for all residents.

1. Infrastructure before expansion: roads built to last, safer layouts, proper traffic management of major routes & local roads with services delivered alongside new housing.

2. Safer communities across Chelmer Ward: tackling speeding and antisocial behaviour, visible policing, and protecting the quiet character of our villages and neighbourhoods.

3. Residents first in every decision: real consultation, transparency, infrastructure that works, and growth planned responsibly with local voices heard from the start.

Smita is the Liberal Democrat local champion to be County Councillor for the Chelmer area (covering Chelmer Village, Boreham, Great Leighs, Beaulieu and most of Channels) is Smita Rajesh. Smita has served as a city councillor for over 6 years and is currently the Deputy Mayor at Chelmsford City Council.

Smita is passionate about serving the community to the best of her abilities. Her top priorities are the welfare of young people, road safety including speed restrictions on school roads and dealing with the pothole crisis, and improving access to healthcare for all.

**Only the Lib Dems can beat the Conservatives and Reform in Chelmsford.**

As a doctor with 18 years of experience, including 8 years as an A&E consultant, I have dedicated my career to standing up for patients and improving healthcare services. I lead and contributed to transforming children’s A&E at one of the Essex Hospitals.

I currently lead the medical examiner service at a major London hospital, ensuring the highest standards of care and accountability.

Throughout my career, I worked on the frontline day and night, including during the COVID-19 pandemic. I have seen firsthand the challenges facing our NHS. That is why I am committed to fighting for my residents to receive the right treatment in the right place. I will campaign tirelessly against unacceptable waiting times and the crisis of corridor care that leaves patients without dignity and proper support.

As a Conservative, I believe in strong leadership, efficiency, and delivering real improvements for our healthcare system and our community.

My wife and I are proud to raise our two children in Chelmsford, where they attend local schools, and I am passionate about ensuring a better future for families like ours.

I will work relentlessly to ensure our healthcare system delivers the timely, high-quality care our residents deserve. Together, we can build a healthier, stronger community.

Vote for your values. Vote Labour. Don’t listen to Lib Dem fearmongering!

Chelmsford West has rejected the Tories. In 2019 they got less than a third of the vote in your ward. You don’t have to ‘play it safe’ and vote tactically. Vote for what you want to see, a Labour council.

Pete has been campaigning in Chelmsford for 25 years. He understands the needs of this changing city. One of the starkest changes he has highlighted is the fire safety issue in high rises and flats. He was campaigning about fire safety in tall bulidings before Grenfell and has escalated this campaign in recent years. The scandal has become as much about financial safety as fire safety for leaseholders and tenants. The government is refusing to change the law. This forces the costs on to the victims rather than those that cause the scandal. #EndOurCladdingScandal​

If elected, I will fight locally to:

Ensure value for money in our council and stop rip-off charges from private contractors and agencies.

Bring further scrutiny to the council building and provide the necessary scrutiny to make sure our council is efficiently run.

Ensure you get the best possible local services for the council tax you pay.

I'm asking you to lend your vote to Reform UK, so I can stand up for Chelmsford Central and fight for real change on your behalf. It is time to put the people of Chelmsford Central first. In this election, you have a voice. Help us build something better for Essex.

Stephen Robinson has a track record as a hard-working Chelmsford campaigner and Councillor. His career has been in communications in the not-for-profit / charity sector.

On Chelmsford City Council, Stephen leads the Liberal Democrats and the Council, where they are working to deliver a greener, fairer and better connected Chelmsford.

Stephen and the Lib Dem team

# have declared a climate and ecology emergency and put in place a climate action plan, including planting 17,000 trees a year

# purchased housing for homeless families and put in place plans to build more

# will ensure that the Local Plan forces developers to invest in local transport, schools, NHS and other services with new housing

# are chasing Conservative-run Essex County Council to invest more in roads and pavement maintenance, not cut the investment budget by £20 million over the next two years.

On Chelmsford Council since 2011, Stephen has led the fight against Conservative plans to cram development into the wrong places without investment in public services. We need housing that is affordable and sustainable, and well paid local jobs.

As well as being a father to my son and daughter, I was a languages
teacher and now teach students with SEND, having worked with
youngsters with disabilities all my adult life.

I am dedicated to ensuring the best possible education and social
care for adults and children throughout Essex, and to improving
education for all our children.

If elected councillor I will bring my experience of advocating for young people to bear on fighting for you and all the people of Essex.

I am standing to represent Chelmsford North because I believe our community deserves practical solutions, transparent leadership, and opportunities that work for everyone—not just a few.

My campaign is built around four priorities that matter deeply to local residents: special education, housing, good governance, and thriving businesses.

Special Education

Every child deserves the support they need to succeed. Too many families in our area struggle to access timely assessments, appropriate placements, and consistent support for children with special educational needs. I will push for increased local funding, better coordination between schools and services, and reduced waiting times for assessments. Parents should feel supported, not exhausted by the system.

Housing

Chelmsford North needs housing that is both affordable and appropriate. I support responsible development that includes genuinely affordable homes, protects green spaces, and invests in the infrastructure our growing community needs. Whether you're a first-time buyer, renter, or long-time resident, housing should be secure, fair, and sustainable.

Governance

Trust in leadership starts with transparency and accountability. I am committed to open decision-making, clear communication, and putting residents at the heart of every policy. Local people should have a real voice in shaping the future of their community, and I will work to ensure that voice is heard and respected.

Businesses and Local Economy

Small and medium-sized businesses are the backbone of our local economy. I will advocate for reducing unnecessary barriers, supporting entrepreneurship, and ensuring our high streets remain vibrant and competitive. By working with local businesses, we can create jobs, boost growth, and keep Chelmsford North thriving.

This campaign is about delivering real change where it matters most. I’m ready to listen, to act, and to serve Chelmsford North with integrity and determination.

Vote Stacy Seales for a

My name is David Wilkinson, and I am standing as your Conservative Party candidate for my home division of Springfield.

I want to do things a little bit differently.

This election will be consequential for the future of Essex and every vote will count. Your postal vote is about to arrive. It will soon be down to you to decide on our future.

For many, running for Councillor is a stepping stone into Westminster politics. Whether driven by a genuine desire to serve the community or by personal gain, this can lead to local issues being pushed aside.

For me, standing for election is my own way of giving my time to my community.

Our current Liberal Democrat County Councillor has been here for over two decades, yet when I have knocked on doors over the last few weeks, the consistent message has been that nobody ever listens, and nothing ever gets fixed. The very job of your local Councillor is to listen and get things fixed, so I invite you to put your faith in me to be the change that you wish to see.

I understand that many local people are toying with the idea of voting for Reform UK. I have seen their literature, and it talks extensively about issues such as immigration and ideology. These issues are not on the ballot at this particular election, and it concerns me that they and their candidates are clearly not focussed on this being a local election.

Whilst the other party’s bicker about the national issues, you can trust me to be in my inbox, focussed on the things that matter to you…

	Pushing for Springfield to receive more of our £12.3 million investment in Essex Highways to resurface roads and pavements, fix potholes and improve drainage. 
	Pushing the Government to honour Conservative plans to for a long-term solution for the Army & Navy and the widening of the A12.
